## Go Anywhere

With 80,000+ Superchargers, Tesla owns and operates the largest global, fast charging network in the world. Located on major routes near convenient amenities, Superchargers keep you charged when you're away from home. Simply plug in, charge and go.

[Find Us](https://www.tesla.com/findus)

# Charging

You have a variety of convenient charging options that can accommodate your lifestyle and driving needs. Whether you’re at home or on the road, you can charge a Tesla vehicle anywhere with access to an outlet.

Where You Park

Discovery - Charging at home from Tesla on Vimeo

![video thumbnail](https://i.vimeocdn.com/video/987083514-80a79a52431ddacbd8dce56e79175fa2bd8ff6365e14a64945fb889cb7b51a04-d?mw=80&q=85)

Playing in picture-in-picture

Play

00:00

00:32

Settings

QualityAuto

Picture-in-Picture

Fullscreen

We recommend charging where you park to meet most of your charging needs.

### Wall Connector

[Wall Connector](https://www.tesla.com/support/charging/wall-connector) offers the fastest charging speed for your home or office, adding [up to 44 miles of range per hour of charge](https://www.tesla.com/support/charging/wall-connector#vehicle-charging-speeds). You can [order a Wall Connector online](https://shop.tesla.com/product/wall-connector) and have it [installed by a Tesla Certified Installer](https://www.tesla.com/support/certified-installers).

### Mobile Connectors and Adapters

If you don’t want to install a Wall Connector, you can [purchase a Mobile Connector](https://shop.tesla.com/product/mobile-connector) and plug into a standard three-prong, 120 volt outlet. A 120 volt outlet will supply 2 to 3 miles of range per hour of charge. If you charge overnight and drive less than 30 to 40 miles per day, this option should meet your typical charging needs.

You can also [purchase an adapter bundle](https://shop.tesla.com/product/nema-adapter-bundle) and charge with other outlet types. Commonly used in homes to power larger appliances, a 240 volt outlet will supply up to 30 miles of range per hour of charge.

### Multi-Unit Buildings

Electric vehicle charging is offered as an amenity at many apartment, condo and office buildings nationwide. Talk to your property manager or HOA about getting chargers installed. We offer the following templates to help get these conversations started with your building manager or HOA:

- [Request your apartment or condo install EV charging](https://digitalassets.tesla.com/tesla-contents/raw/upload/EV_Installation_Apartment_Condo_Request.docx)
- [Request for approval to self-install EV charging in your deeded parking space](https://digitalassets.tesla.com/tesla-contents/raw/upload/EV_Installation_HOA_Request.docx)

For property managers and owners, Commercial Charging provides a self-service option to install shared charging stations for tenants and guests. Find information on [how to host Commercial Charging](https://www.tesla.com/support/charging/commercial) at your property.

[Back to Top](https://www.tesla.com/support/charging#top)

On the Road

Discovery - Charging on the road from Tesla on Vimeo

![video thumbnail](https://i.vimeocdn.com/video/2005075281-6f731fa060933752555c7eeeb69276b216adce0ac745667add55ebfa10ba1d1e-d?mw=80&q=85)

When you’re traveling in your Tesla vehicle or away from where you normally park, there are three main charging options to keep your vehicle charged on the go.

### Superchargers

[Superchargers](https://www.tesla.com/supercharger) are the fastest charging option when you’re away from home, allowing you to charge your vehicle up to 200 miles in 15 minutes. Designed to get you charged and back on the road as quickly as possible, we own and operate over [80,000 global Superchargers](https://www.tesla.com/findus?filters=supercharger) that are accessible on a 24/7 basis, located on major routes near convenient amenities.

### Destination Charging

[Destination Charging locations](https://www.tesla.com/destination-charging) are comprised of popular hotels, restaurants, vineyards, resorts and other destinations that host Wall Connectors for customers to charge. There are over [40,000 Wall Connectors at Destination Charging locations](https://www.tesla.com/findus?filters=destination%20charger) in urban areas and rural locations, making it easy to charge when you arrive at your location or have a few hours to spare.

### Third-Party Charging

Third-party charging stations can be located through [Plugshare.com](https://www.plugshare.com/). Charging stations labeled as J1772 will work with a SAE J1772 adapter and 120 volt stations labeled as 120 volt will work with the Mobile Connector. When a Mobile Connector is not in use, we recommend storing it in your vehicle for easy access. For maximum flexibility on the road and to charge from a 240 volt outlet, store an [adapter bundle](https://shop.tesla.com/product/nema-adapter-bundle) in your vehicle as well.

[Back to Top](https://www.tesla.com/support/charging#top)

Charge Speed Comparison

Each charging option provides different charge speeds. To view the max amperage of your Tesla vehicle, tap the lightning bolt icon on the touchscreen. To determine the max amperage of your electric vehicle, refer to your vehicle manufacturer’s guidance.

| Hardware | Charge Speed |
| --- | --- |
| Wall Connector<br> ![Wall Connector](https://digitalassets.tesla.com/tesla-contents/image/upload/f_auto,q_auto/Home-Charging-Wall-Connector) | Up to 44 miles of range per hour of charge<br>[Order Now](https://shop.tesla.com/product/wall-connector) |
| Mobile Connector<br> ![Mobile Connector](https://digitalassets.tesla.com/tesla-contents/image/upload/f_auto,q_auto/Home-Charging-Mobile-Connector) | Up to 3 miles of range per hour of charge<br>[Order Now](https://shop.tesla.com/product/mobile-connector) |
| Supercharger<br> ![Supercharger](https://digitalassets.tesla.com/tesla-contents/image/upload/f_auto,q_auto/Supercharger.png) | Up to 200 miles in 15 minutes of charge<br>[Find a Supercharger](https://www.tesla.com/findus?filters=supercharger) |
| Destination Charging<br> ![Destination Charger](https://digitalassets-shop.tesla.com/image/upload/f_auto,q_auto/v1/content/dam/tesla/CAR_ACCESSORIES/MODEL_3/CHARGING_ADAPTERS/1508484-00-B_0.jpg) | Up to 44 miles of range per hour of charge<br>[Find Destination Charging Locations](https://www.tesla.com/findus?filters=destination%20charger) |

[Back to Top](https://www.tesla.com/support/charging#top)

How to Charge

To charge your Tesla vehicle, you first need to open the charge port using your vehicle’s touchscreen or the Tesla app. You can also open the charge port by pressing the bottom of the charge port door while your vehicle is unlocked.

Plug the charging connector into your vehicle charge port. The charge port LED will flash green when charging has begun. You can monitor your charging progress in the [Tesla app](https://www.tesla.com/support/tesla-app).

When you’re done charging, disconnect the charging cable by holding the button on the connector handle to release the latch and pulling the handle from the charge port. The charge port will automatically close.

[Back to Top](https://www.tesla.com/support/charging#top)

Plan Your Trip

Discover: Trip Planner from Tesla on Vimeo

![video thumbnail](https://i.vimeocdn.com/video/993631549-c4ad708d62dbcbd9c3d603f14d1f3d6cf4e804ab3657578d3d1cbeae10ef626c-d?mw=80&q=85)

When you’re traveling, the Trip Planner on your vehicle touchscreen will guide you to your destination. Simply enter your end location on the touchscreen and the touchscreen will zoom out to provide an overview of the route as well as the most convenient Superchargers along the way. We advise using your Trip Planner when you’re on the road or traveling long distances so that it can provide the most accurate recommended stops and charge times based on your driving.

Certain high data usage vehicle features require at least Standard Connectivity, including maps, navigation and voice commands. Access to features that use cellular data and third-party licenses are subject to change. [Learn more about Standard Connectivity and any limitations](https://www.tesla.com/support/connectivity#connectivity-features-limited).

[Back to Top](https://www.tesla.com/support/charging#top)

Installation Incentives

Incentives to install EV charging equipment exist at the federal, state and local level, as well as from many utility companies.

[Find available incentives in your area](https://www.tesla.com/support/incentives#state-local-and-utility-incentives) and visit your local government and utility websites for additional programs available to you.

[Back to Top](https://www.tesla.com/support/charging#top)

Report an Issue

If you are experiencing an issue before or during a charging session at a Supercharger or a Destination Charging location, you can report the issue from the Tesla app. To report an issue, follow these steps:

1. Open the Tesla app.
2. Select a location.
3. Find and select ‘Report an Issue.’
4. Select the category and include a photo.

**Note:** To access this feature, you must have Tesla app version 4.39.0 or later. Make sure you have the latest version of the app by updating the Tesla app on your mobile device.

Our team will review the issue and determine the next steps. Although our systems can detect issues that can affect charging performance, reports of onsite issues such as access, cleanliness or damage to charging stations will be investigated from the report.

[Back to Top](https://www.tesla.com/support/charging#top)

Provide Feedback

You will receive prompts in the Tesla app to complete our charging survey to provide feedback. You can rate your charging experience out of five stars and explain your rating. This rating allows us to understand how satisfied you are with the Tesla Charging experience.

[Back to Top](https://www.tesla.com/support/charging#top)

In-Person Workshop

Tesla now offers in-person workshops at select Tesla locations for those interested in experiencing Tesla products and features. Sign up for our workshops to learn more about charging options for your Tesla vehicle.

[Sign Up](https://www.tesla.com/events/charging "Sign Up")

[Back to Top](https://www.tesla.com/support/charging#top)
